name.es: Desem (pan integral de masa madre flamenco)

description.en: A rare Flemish tradition: 100% whole wheat leavened by a starter kept cold and fed only on fresh-milled flour, never store yeast. The result is surprisingly mild, sweetly aromatic bread, nothing like the sharp tang of San Francisco sourdough.
description.de: Eine seltene flämische Tradition: reines Weizenvollkornbrot, gesäuert mit einem kühl gehaltenen Sauerteig, der nur mit frisch gemahlenem Mehl gefüttert wird. Das Ergebnis ist überraschend mild und süßlich-aromatisch, ganz anders als der scharfe San-Francisco-Sauerteig.
description.es: Una tradición flamenca poco común: pan 100% integral fermentado con un cultivo mantenido en frío y alimentado solo con harina recién molida. El resultado es sorprendentemente suave y dulcemente aromático, muy distinto del sabor ácido de la masa madre de San Francisco.
description.fr: Une tradition flamande rare: du pain 100% complet levé par un levain maintenu au frais et nourri uniquement de farine fraîchement moulue. Le résultat est étonnamment doux et aromatique, rien à voir avec l'acidité franche du levain de San Francisco.

origin.note: Popularized outside Belgium by Laurel Robertson's 'Laurel's Kitchen Bread Book'; the starter is grown in cool flour rather than inoculated from the air.

ingredients.note: hard white spring wheat, freshly milled
Red Fife heritage wheat, freshly milled
mature desem culture, kept cool and fed only on flour

keeping.note: Improves a day after baking as the whole-grain crumb settles.

method.title: Maintain the desem culture
method.summary: Keep a whole-wheat-only starter buried in a cool bed of flour (10-18°C), fed every few days; it draws its leavening purely from organisms in the grain, never a commercial yeast.

method.title: Build the levain
method.summary: The day before baking, mix a portion of desem culture with fresh-milled flour and water; let it ripen 8-12 hours.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Combine the ripe levain with the remaining freshly milled whole wheat flours, water and salt into a shaggy dough.

method.title: Bulk ferment
method.summary: Rest 20-30 minutes, then work through a few stretch-and-folds over 4-8 hours until the dough has expanded by about 90%.

method.title: Shape and proof
method.summary: Shape into a tight boule and proof either 1-2 hours at room temperature or, for deeper flavor, 20-40 minutes on the counter followed by an overnight cold retard.

method.title: Bake hot, then step down
method.summary: Bake in a well-preheated covered pot: very hot initially, then reduce twice, finishing uncovered for a dark crust and a hollow sound when tapped.

method.title: Cool fully
method.summary: Let the loaf rest at least 2 hours before cutting so the dense, all-whole-grain crumb finishes setting.

source.adaptation: Percentages recomputed to include the levain's flour and water in the 100% flour base per house convention.
